lenses.guidance: The four coverings (vv 1-14) layer outward from holiness toward weather; the two veils (vv 31-37) gate inward toward holiness. Architectural narrative: the structure is a graded approach to presence.

The inner veil (vv 31-33) separates Holy Place from Most Holy. The pattern tears at Mark 15:38 from top to bottom; Heb 10:19-20 names Christ's flesh the new way through. The chapter's veil is built to be torn.

The cherubim woven into the inner veil (v 31) match the ark's cherubim (25:18) — guards on both sides of the boundary. Christ's body torn (Heb 10:20) removes the guard; access is opened by his flesh, not by ours.
